patches.hypothesis: The root cause of the issue is the incorrect handling of the offset when it is zero. The previous fix changed the condition to handle zero offsets but did not address the underlying logic for ensuring the correct offset during DST transitions. We need to ensure that the offset is correctly adjusted even when it is zero, and handle both forward and backward DST transitions properly.
patches.original_hypothesis: The issue arises because the method does not correctly handle the transition from British Summer Time (BST) to Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) on the specified date. The method might be incorrectly calculating the offset for the given local time, leading to an incorrect UTC conversion.
